steps: Go to https://supabase.com/dashboard and log in or create a free account
Create a new project by clicking 'New project', entering a name, database password, and region
Wait for the project to finish provisioning (about 1-2 minutes)
Go to your project's Settings > API (or visit https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/YOUR_PROJECT_REF/settings/api)
Copy your Project URL — the Auth API base URL is https://YOUR_PROJECT_REF.supabase.co/auth/v1
Copy the 'anon public' key for client-side auth operations, or 'service_role secret' for admin operations (admin_users_list, admin_user_update, etc.)

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key (anon or service_role)
credential_fields.description: Use the 'anon' key for client-facing auth (sign up, sign in) or the 'service_role' key for admin operations (list/update/delete users). Passed in the 'apikey' header.

notes: Free tier available (2 active projects, up to 50,000 monthly active users)
The actual Auth API base URL is https://YOUR_PROJECT_REF.supabase.co/auth/v1 — not api.supabase.io
Client-side operations (sign_up, sign_in_password, etc.) require the 'anon' key
Admin operations (admin_users_list, admin_user_update, admin_user_delete) require the 'service_role' key
The 'service_role' key bypasses all Row Level Security — never expose it in client-side code
Authenticated user operations also require passing the user's JWT in the 'Authorization: Bearer <jwt>' header

test_endpoint.description: Check Auth service health to verify the project URL is correct
